As the patch size decreases in FlexiViT, each token splits into multiple tokens. The cosine similarity between token representations at different patch sizes is measured to find correspondences.

Ensembling multiple predictions of the same FlexiViT at different scales is explored to improve prediction accuracy. It is found that running a single FlexiViT at a specific compute budget is more efficient than ensembling multiple smaller ones.
